codeReview.summary: The deferred DeltaNet allreduce investigation is thorough, well-documented, and correctly concluded. The test (test_deferred_allreduce.py) properly measures both the allreduce count reduction (128 → 80, 37.5% reduction) and the numerical accuracy impact (cosine sim = 0.589, far below 0.99 threshold). The feature spec explicitly required: 'If cosine sim < 0.99, do NOT force it. Report the finding and skip this optimization.' The worker followed this instruction correctly. No production code was changed, only test and documentation were added.

issues.description: The performance benchmark for deferred mode uses the serial (non-cached) dispatch path, which benchmarks at 14.4 tok/s vs standard combined mode's 33.6 tok/s, giving a 0.42x speedup. This comparison is misleading because deferred mode doesn't have a cached dispatch implementation (it was intentionally not built since it's infeasible). A note in the benchmark output clarifies this, but the comparison isn't apples-to-apples.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md was updated with the deferred DeltaNet allreduce finding, including measured cosine similarity (0.589), allreduce count analysis, and the architectural reason for the divergence. This is good documentation for future workers who might revisit this optimization.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit ed10864 adds 44 lines to .factory/library/architecture.md with the finding labeled 'DeltaNet Deferred Allreduce: INFEASIBLE'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The investigation correctly followed the feature spec contract: measure first, implement only if cosine sim > 0.99. The worker did not attempt to implement or force an approximation when the threshold wasn't met. This is the right pattern for numerical feasibility checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test_deferred_allreduce.py correctly uses COSINE_SIM_THRESHOLD = 0.99 and reports INFEASIBLE when min_cosine_sim = 0.589 < 0.99.

summary: PASS. The deferred DeltaNet allreduce investigation is correctly conducted and documented. The feature found the optimization infeasible (cosine sim 0.589 < 0.99 threshold) and correctly skipped implementation per spec. VAL-ADV-002 is satisfied per the updated validation contract ('INVESTIGATED AND FOUND INFEASIBLE'). One minor non-blocking issue: the performance comparison in the benchmark is not apples-to-apples (serial deferred vs combined standard). Architecture.md was correctly updated with the finding and rationale.
